## BPSC 72nd Combined Competitive Examination (CCE) 2026: Upcoming Notification

The Bihar Public Service Commission (BPSC) is gearing up to release the notification for its 72nd Combined Competitive Examination (CCE) for the year 2026. This prestigious examination is conducted annually to recruit eligible candidates for various administrative and civil service posts across different departments of the Bihar State Government. Aspiring candidates are advised to regularly check the official BPSC website for the latest updates regarding this significant recruitment drive.

### Important Dates

All dates will be announced with the official BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 notification.

| Event | Date | Status |
| :---------------------------------- | :---------- | :---------------------------------- |
| Official Notification Release Date | To be announced | Awaiting Release |
| Online Application Start Date | To be announced | Awaiting Release |
| Online Application End Date | To be announced | Awaiting Release |
| Last Date for Fee Payment | To be announced | Awaiting Release |
| Prelims Exam Date | To be announced | Awaiting Release |
| Mains Exam Date | To be announced | Awaiting Release |
| Interview Dates | To be announced | Awaiting Release |

### Application Fee

Specific application fees for the BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 will be detailed in the official notification. Candidates are advised to refer to the official document for accurate fee structures and payment methods.

| Category | Application Fee | Payment Mode |
| :------------------------ | :-------------- | :-------------------------------- |
| General/OBC | To be announced | Online (Net Banking/Debit/Credit Card) |
| SC/ST/PwBD | To be announced | Online (Net Banking/Debit/Credit Card) |
| Female (Bihar Domicile) | To be announced | Online (Net Banking/Debit/Credit Card) |

### Vacancy Details

The exact number of vacancies for the BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 will be announced along with the official notification. The recruitment is expected to cover various Group 'A' and Group 'B' posts in different state government departments. Details regarding post-wise vacancies will be updated here once officially released.

### Eligibility Criteria

Detailed eligibility criteria, including educational qualifications and nationality, will be specified in the official BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 notification. Typically, candidates are required to hold a Bachelor's Degree from a recognized university.

#### Post-wise Qualifications

Specific educational qualifications for different posts (e.g., Sub Divisional Officer, Rural Development Officer, etc.) will be outlined in the official notification. Generally, a Bachelor's degree in any discipline from a recognized university or equivalent institution is the minimum requirement.

### Age Limit (as on a specific cut-off date)

The age limit for candidates applying for the BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 will be released with the official notification. Age relaxation for reserved categories (SC/ST/OBC/PwBD) will be provided as per Bihar Government rules. The minimum and maximum age criteria will be determined based on the cut-off date mentioned in the notification.

*   **Minimum Age:** To be announced
*   **Maximum Age (General/Unreserved Male):** To be announced
*   **Age Relaxation:** Applicable as per Government rules for reserved categories.

### Selection Process

BPSC Combined Competitive Examination typically involves a three-stage selection process:

1.  **Preliminary Examination (Prelims):** A qualifying objective type test.
2.  **Main Examination (Mains):** A descriptive type examination, scores of which are considered for the final merit list.
3.  **Interview:** A personality test for candidates who qualify the Mains examination.

### How to Apply

Detailed step-by-step instructions for applying online for the BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 will be provided in the official notification. Candidates are advised to follow these steps carefully to ensure a successful application submission. The general process is as follows:

1.  **Visit the Official Website:** Go to the official website of the Bihar Public Service Commission (bpsc.bih.nic.in).
2.  **Find the Notification:** Look for the 'BPSC 72nd CCE 2026 Notification' link under the 'Latest Advertisements' section.
3.  **Read the Notification:** Download and thoroughly read the official notification to understand all details, eligibility criteria, and instructions.
4.  **Online Registration:** Click on the 'Apply Online' link and complete the registration process by providing basic details.
5.  **Fill Application Form:** Log in with your registration credentials and fill out the detailed application form with personal, educational, and other required information.
6.  **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your photograph, signature, and other necessary documents as per the specified format and size.
7.  **Pay Application Fee:** Pay the requisite application fee online through the available payment gateway.
8.  **Review and Submit:** Review all the entered information carefully before final submission. Make sure there are no errors.
9.  **Print Application:** After successful submission, take a printout of the application form for future reference.

### Important Links

*   **Official BPSC Website:** [https://www.bpsc.bih.nic.in/](https://www.bpsc.bih.nic.in/)

## Army Agniveer Admit Card 2026 Released

The Indian Army has announced the release of Admit Cards for the Agniveer recruitment cycle of 2026. This is a crucial step for all aspirants who have successfully applied for the Agniveer scheme and are preparing for the upcoming Computer Based Test (CBT). Candidates must download their admit cards from the official portal to gain entry to the examination centers.

### Important Dates

| Event                       | Date                                    |
| :-------------------------- | :-------------------------------------- |
| Application Period          | *Refer to original notification*        |
| **Admit Card Release Date** | **Available Now**                       |
| **Exam Date (CBT)**         | **To be mentioned on your Admit Card**  |
| Result Declaration          | To Be Announced                         |

### Admit Card Download Process

Candidates are required to download their admit cards exclusively from the official Indian Army recruitment portal. A printed copy of the admit card is mandatory for entry to the examination center. For detailed, step-by-step instructions on how to download your admit card, please refer to the 'How to Apply' section above.

### Required Documents for Exam Day

On the day of the examination, candidates must carry the following essential documents to the examination center:

*   **Printed Admit Card:** A clear, legible printout of the Army Agniveer Admit Card 2026.
*   **Original Photo ID Proof:** A valid government-issued photo identification such as Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Voter ID, Passport, or Driving License. The ID must match the details on the admit card.
*   **Passport Size Photographs:** Two recent passport-sized photographs (preferably the same as those uploaded during the online application process).

### Exam Day Guidelines

To ensure a fair, secure, and disciplined examination process, candidates are advised to strictly adhere to the following guidelines:

*   **Reporting Time:** Arrive at the examination center well in advance of the specified reporting time to allow for necessary verification procedures, including biometric attendance and frisking.
*   **Prohibited Items:** Do not carry any electronic gadgets (mobile phones, smartwatches, Bluetooth devices, calculators), study material, books, notes, or any other unauthorized items into the examination hall. Any such items will lead to disqualification.
*   **Rough Work:** Rough sheets will be provided at the examination center for calculations and rough work. Do not bring your own rough sheets.
*   **Biometric Verification:** Cooperate fully with the invigilators and staff for biometric attendance and photograph capture at the entry point.
*   **Instructions:** Carefully read and strictly follow all instructions mentioned on your admit card and those given by the invigilators during the examination.
*   **COVID-19 Protocols:** Adhere to all prevailing COVID-19 related guidelines, including maintaining social distancing, wearing masks, and using hand sanitizers, if applicable.

*   [Direct Link to Download Admit Card](Direct download link will be updated here)

content:
## India Post Office GDS Result 2026 (1st Merit List) Released

Good news for aspirants who applied for the India Post Gramin Dak Sevak (GDS) recruitment! The India Post Office has officially released the 1st Merit List for the 2026 cycle. This provisional selection list is a crucial step for candidates vying for the GDS posts across various circles.

The Gramin Dak Sevak role is a vital part of the Indian postal network, primarily responsible for tasks like selling stamps and stationery, delivering mail, and other related services in rural areas. The selection for GDS posts is typically based on the merit of marks obtained in the 10th standard, making this merit list a direct reflection of academic performance.

### Important Dates & Timelines

Candidates should pay close attention to the following tentative schedule for the result and subsequent document verification process. Please note that specific dates will be announced by India Post for each circle.

| Event                           | Tentative Date / Status       |
| :------------------------------ | :---------------------------- |
| GDS 2026 Application Period     | To be announced / Concluded   |
| **1st Merit List Release Date** | **Released (As per title)**   |
| Document Verification (DV) Start| Soon after merit list release |
| DV Last Date                    | To be announced               |
| Final Merit List                | Post DV completion            |

### How to Download the 1st Merit List

To check your selection status and download the 1st Merit List PDF, candidates are advised to follow these general steps. Please ensure you refer to the official India Post GDS website for the exact links and instructions for your respective postal circle:

1.  **Visit the Official GDS Portal**: Navigate to the official website dedicated to India Post GDS recruitment.
2.  **Locate 'Result' Section**: On the homepage, look for a section specifically labeled 'Results' or 'Candidate's Corner'.
3.  **Click on GDS 2026 Merit List**: Find and click the link corresponding to 'Gramin Dak Sevak 2026 - 1st Merit List'.
4.  **Select Your Circle**: You will likely be prompted to select your postal circle (state/region) for which you applied.
5.  **Download PDF**: The merit list will appear as a downloadable PDF file. Click to open or save it.
6.  **Search Your Details**: Open the downloaded PDF and use the search function (Ctrl+F) to find your Registration Number or Name to confirm your provisional selection.

### Document Verification (DV) Process

Candidates whose names appear in the 1st Merit List will be provisionally selected and called for the Document Verification process. It is crucial to have all original documents ready, along with self-attested copies, as specified in the original recruitment notification. Common documents required include:

*   10th Class Mark Sheet/Certificate
*   Date of Birth Proof (if not on 10th certificate)
*   Caste Certificate (if applicable)
*   EWS Certificate (if applicable)
*   Disability Certificate (if applicable)
*   Computer Knowledge Certificate (if required and mentioned in notification)
*   Medical Certificate
*   Photo ID proof (Aadhar Card, PAN Card, etc.)
*   Recent Passport-sized Photographs

Detailed instructions regarding the DV schedule, venue, and required documents will be communicated by the respective Postal Circles through official channels. Candidates are advised to regularly check the official website for updates and prepare thoroughly for this essential stage of the recruitment process.

### Selection Process
The selection will be conducted through the 72nd Combined Competitive Examination (CCE) in three successive stages:
1. **Stage-I: Preliminary Examination** – Objective Type (Screening Test). Only qualified candidates will proceed to Main Examination.
2. **Stage-II: Main Examination** – Descriptive / Written Examination (conventional type papers).
3. **Stage-III: Interview / Personality Test** – For candidates who qualify the Main Examination.
4. Document Verification & Medical Examination (as per BPSC rules).
5. Final merit list will be prepared on the basis of marks obtained in Main Examination + Interview. Preliminary Examination is only of qualifying nature.

### How to Apply
Eligible & Interested candidates must apply online only through the BPSC Online Application Portal.
1. Visit the official BPSC website [https://bpsc.bihar.gov.in](https://bpsc.bihar.gov.in) and go to the Online Application Portal (bpsconline.bihar.gov.in).
2. Complete One Time Registration (OTR) – only one OTR is allowed per candidate.
3. Login and fill the online application form carefully with personal, educational, category, and other details.
4. Upload all required documents in PDF format (max 100 KB each), live webcam photograph, scanned signatures (Hindi & English), and thumb impression.
5. Pay the application fee + biometric fee (if applicable) online.
6. Submit the form and take a printout of the finally submitted application form for future reference. Keep the Application Number safe.

### Important Links
- [BPSC Official Website](https://bpsc.bihar.gov.in)
- [Apply Online](https://bpsc.bihar.gov.in)
- [Latest All India Government Jobs 2026](https://www.indgovtjobs.in)

44 Sugar Cane Officer posts under 72nd CCE (UR-18, EWS-4, EBC-8, BC-5, SC-7, ST-2).

Age is calculated as on 01.08.2026. UR (Male): 37 years, OBC/Female: 40 years, SC/ST: 42 years (minimum age 20/21/22 years as applicable) with relaxations as per rules.

1. **Revise Key Concepts**: Focus on important topics and formulas.

## Mumbai University Second Merit List 2026

Mumbai University has officially declared the Second Merit List for the academic session 2026-27 on **30th May 2026**. Candidates who have applied for various undergraduate and postgraduate programs can now check their selection status online.

### Important Dates

| Event | Date |
|-------|------|
| Release of Second Merit List | 30-05-2026 |
| Document Verification & Fee Payment | 31-05-2026 to 05-06-2026 |
| Commencement of Classes | 15-06-2026 |

### How to Check the Merit List

1. Visit the official Mumbai University admission portal: [mu.ac.in](https://mu.ac.in)
2. Click on the 'Admission 2026-27' section.
3. Select 'Second Merit List' for your respective course.
4. Enter your **Application Number** and **Date of Birth**.
5. Click on 'Submit' to view your status.
6. Download and take a printout of the merit list for future reference.

### Documents Required for Verification

- Printout of the merit list
- Application form confirmation page
- Mark sheets of qualifying examination (10th, 12th, Graduation, etc.)
- Transfer Certificate (TC) / Migration Certificate
- Caste/Category certificate (if applicable)
- Income certificate (if applicable)
- Passport-size photographs
- Photo ID proof (Aadhaar, PAN, etc.)

### Cut-Off Marks

The cut-off marks for the Second Merit List vary by course and category. Candidates are advised to check the specific cut-off for their program on the official website.

### Important Notes

- Candidates who are selected must complete document verification and fee payment within the stipulated dates.
- Failure to do so will result in cancellation of the allotted seat.
- The university reserves the right to modify the schedule if necessary.

For more details, visit the official website: [mu.ac.in](https://mu.ac.in)
